actors.name: User (Superfluid)
actors.description: Individual or entity interacting with the Superfluid protocol to manage Super Tokens, create streams, or participate in distributions. Interacts via a wallet and DApp/Client Application. (doc_2, doc_13, doc_44)
capabilities: Deploy Super Tokens and Super Apps (if also a Developer)
Stake in TOGA (if also a PIC)

actors.name: Sentinel
actors.description: Operator of off-chain software that monitors the Superfluid network and liquidates critical or insolvent streams. (doc_1, doc_19, doc_43)

actors.name: Pool Admin
actors.description: An account (EOA or contract) responsible for creating and managing a specific Superfluid Distribution Pool, including assigning units to members. (doc_5, doc_28, doc_30)
capabilities: Update member units in a pool
Distribute funds/streams to a pool (if configured)

actors.name: Super App
actors.description: A smart contract registered with the Superfluid Protocol that can react to Superfluid agreement actions (e.g., stream creation/updates) via callbacks. (doc_3, doc_24, doc_21)
capabilities: Execute custom logic upon receiving Superfluid callbacks
Interact with Superfluid Host and Agreements

actors.name: Developer
actors.description: Individual or entity that deploys Super Tokens, Super Apps, or other smart contracts interacting with the Superfluid protocol. (doc_41, doc_42, doc_22)

actors.name: PIC (Patrician in Charge)
actors.description: An account that stakes Super Tokens in the TOGA contract to become eligible for rewards from liquidating critical streams during the patrician period. (doc_1, doc_18)
capabilities: Stake Super Tokens in TOGA contract

actors.name: Superfluid DAO Participant
actors.description: Holder of Superfluid governance tokens (e.g., SUP) participating in protocol governance decisions. (doc_11)
capabilities: Propose changes to the protocol (depending on governance rules)

assets.name: Super Token
assets.description: A programmable token standard extending ERC20/ERC777, enabling features like money streaming and distributions. Can be Wrapper, Pure, or Native Asset type. (doc_7, doc_39)

assets.name: Underlying ERC20 Token
assets.description: A standard ERC20 token that can be wrapped to create a Wrapper Super Token. (doc_7, doc_42)

assets.name: Native Blockchain Asset
assets.description: The native currency of a blockchain (e.g., ETH, MATIC) that can be wrapped to create a Native Asset Super Token. (doc_39, doc_58)

assets.name: Stream Buffer/Deposit
assets.description: An amount of Super Tokens locked by a stream sender in the CFAv1 agreement as a security deposit to ensure stream solvency. (doc_1, doc_6, doc_18)

assets.name: TOGA Stake
assets.description: Super Tokens staked by a PIC in the TOGA contract to participate in the liquidation auction. (doc_1, doc_18)

assets.name: Pool Units
assets.description: Represents a member's proportional share in a GDAv1 Distribution Pool. Can be an ERC20-like representation within the pool contract. (doc_1, doc_5, doc_28, doc_30)

assets.name: User-provided Data (in transactions)
assets.description: Arbitrary byte data that can be attached to Superfluid operations (e.g., creating a stream, distributing to a pool) for custom logic or off-chain interpretation. (doc_1, doc_37, doc_51)

assets.name: Existential NFT
assets.description: Non-Fungible Tokens representing specific roles or states within the Superfluid protocol, such as ConstantFlowNFT for streams, PoolAdminNFT for pool administration rights, or PoolMemberNFT for pool membership. (doc_61)

components.name: User Wallet
components.description: Software or hardware used by actors (User, Developer, Pool Admin, PIC, DAO Participant) to manage keys and sign transactions for interacting with the Berachain network and Superfluid contracts. (doc_13, doc_44)

components.name: DApp/Client Application
components.description: Frontend interface (e.g., Superfluid Dashboard, custom web application) used by users to interact with Superfluid Forwarder contracts or directly with protocol contracts. (doc_44, doc_49)

components.name: Superfluid Host Contract
components.description: The central UUPS proxy contract of the Superfluid protocol. It manages agreement registrations, Super App callbacks, and routes calls to appropriate agreement contracts. (doc_4, doc_61)

components.name: Super Token Contract
components.description: UUPS proxy contract implementing the ISuperToken interface. Manages the logic for a specific Super Token (Wrapper, Pure, or Native Asset type), including real-time balance calculations and wrap/unwrap operations. (doc_7, doc_39, doc_59, doc_58)

components.name: Constant Flow Agreement V1 (CFAv1) Contract
components.description: UUPS proxy contract managing the logic for creating, updating, and deleting continuous money streams (flows). (doc_1, doc_6, doc_61)

components.name: General Distribution Agreement V1 (GDAv1) Contract
components.description: UUPS proxy contract managing the logic for creating distribution pools and distributing Super Tokens (instantly or as streams) to pool members based on their units. (doc_5, doc_28, doc_61)

components.name: Super Token Factory Contract
components.description: UUPS proxy contract used to deploy new Super Token contracts (Wrapper or Pure types). (doc_41, doc_42, doc_61)

components.name: CFAv1Forwarder Contract
components.description: An immutable helper contract that simplifies client-side interactions with the CFAv1 for creating, updating, and deleting flows, and managing permissions. (doc_50, doc_56)

components.name: GDAv1Forwarder Contract
components.description: An immutable helper contract that simplifies client-side interactions with the GDAv1 for creating pools, managing members, distributing tokens, and claiming from pools. (doc_46, doc_47, doc_57)

components.name: Superfluid Subgraph
components.description: An off-chain indexing service (The Graph node) that processes Superfluid contract events, providing a queryable GraphQL API for protocol data. (doc_48, doc_52, doc_62)

components.name: TOGA Contract
components.description: UUPS proxy contract managing the Transparent Ongoing Auction for PIC selection and stake management. (doc_1, doc_18)

components.name: Sentinel Node Software
components.description: Off-chain software run by Sentinels to monitor stream solvency and submit liquidation transactions to the CFAv1 contract via the Superfluid Host. (doc_19)

components.name: SuperfluidPool Contract
components.description: A UUPS proxy contract instance representing a specific distribution pool, created via GDAv1. It manages member units and distributes received tokens. (doc_28, doc_60, doc_61)

components.name: MacroForwarder Contract
components.description: An immutable, trusted forwarder contract that allows users to execute pre-defined batch operations (macros) on the Superfluid protocol. (doc_45)

components.name: UserDefinedMacro Contract
components.description: A custom smart contract deployed by a developer that defines a specific set of batch operations to be executed via the MacroForwarder. (doc_45)

components.name: SuperfluidGovernanceII Contract
components.description: The UUPS proxy contract responsible for the governance of the Superfluid protocol, allowing DAO participants to vote on proposals. (doc_11, doc_61)

components.name: Existential NFT Contract
components.description: UUPS proxy contract implementing logic for Superfluid NFTs (ConstantFlowNFT, PoolAdminNFT, PoolMemberNFT) which represent ownership or roles. (doc_61)

data_flows.data: User intent to perform action (e.g., create stream, wrap token)
data_flows.description: User interacts with their wallet to authorize a transaction. (Implicit)

data_flows.data: Signed transaction or connection request
data_flows.description: User's wallet communicates with a DApp/Client Application. (Implicit)

data_flows.data: Transaction call to create/update/delete stream (e.g., `createFlow` with Super Token, sender, receiver, flowrate, userData). (doc_50)
data_flows.description: DApp sends request to CFAv1Forwarder for stream management.

data_flows.data: Transaction call to create pool, update units, distribute, or claim (e.g., `createPool` with Super Token, admin, config). (doc_47, doc_46)
data_flows.description: DApp sends request to GDAv1Forwarder for pool management.

data_flows.data: Transaction call to wrap/unwrap Super Tokens (e.g., `upgrade(amount)` or `downgrade(amount)`). (doc_55)
data_flows.description: DApp sends request to Super Token contract for wrapping/unwrapping.

data_flows.data: Forwarded call for CFAv1 operations (e.g., `callAgreement` for createFlow). (doc_56, doc_4)
data_flows.description: CFAv1Forwarder calls the Superfluid Host.

data_flows.data: Forwarded call for GDAv1 operations (e.g., `callAgreement` for createPool). (doc_57, doc_4)
data_flows.description: GDAv1Forwarder calls the Superfluid Host.

data_flows.data: Execute CFAv1 logic (create/update/delete flow, permissioning). (doc_4)
data_flows.description: Superfluid Host routes call to CFAv1 contract.

data_flows.data: Execute GDAv1 logic (create pool, distribute, claim). (doc_4)
data_flows.description: Superfluid Host routes call to GDAv1 contract.

data_flows.data: Update Super Token state (lock/release buffer, adjust real-time balance). (doc_7, doc_13)
data_flows.description: CFAv1 interacts with Super Token contract for stream operations.

data_flows.data: Create or interact with a SuperfluidPool instance (e.g., update units, trigger distribution). (doc_28, doc_29)
data_flows.description: GDAv1 interacts with a specific SuperfluidPool contract.

data_flows.data: Transfer Super Tokens to/from pool members. (doc_28)
data_flows.description: SuperfluidPool contract interacts with Super Token contract for distributions/claims.

data_flows.data: Callback invocation (e.g., `afterAgreementCreated`) with context and user data. (doc_3, doc_21)
data_flows.description: Superfluid Host calls a registered Super App upon specific agreement actions.

data_flows.data: Super App calls Superfluid Host (e.g., `callAgreementWithContext`). (doc_21)
data_flows.description: Super App interacts back with the Superfluid Host to perform actions.

data_flows.data: Liquidation transaction for an insolvent stream. (doc_18, doc_19)
data_flows.description: Sentinel Node Software (via an EOA) sends a transaction to the Superfluid Host to call CFAv1's liquidation function.

data_flows.data: Transaction to stake/unstake Super Tokens for PIC role. (doc_18)
data_flows.description: PIC (via User Wallet) interacts with TOGA contract.

data_flows.data: GraphQL query for Superfluid protocol data. (doc_48, doc_52)
data_flows.description: DApp/Client Application queries the Superfluid Subgraph.

data_flows.data: Query results (protocol data). (doc_48, doc_52)
data_flows.description: Superfluid Subgraph returns data to DApp/Client Application.

data_flows.data: Transaction to deploy a new Super Token contract. (doc_41, doc_42)
data_flows.description: Developer (via User Wallet) calls Super Token Factory.

data_flows.data: Transaction to execute a macro (e.g., `runMacro` with UserDefinedMacro address and params). (doc_45)
data_flows.description: User (via User Wallet) calls MacroForwarder.

data_flows.data: Call to `buildBatchOperations` with params. (doc_45)
data_flows.description: MacroForwarder calls the specified UserDefinedMacro contract.

data_flows.data: Returns array of `ISuperfluid.Operation` structs. (doc_45)
data_flows.description: UserDefinedMacro returns batch operations to MacroForwarder.

data_flows.data: Forwarded batch call (`forwardBatchCall`). (doc_45)
data_flows.description: MacroForwarder sends batch operations to Superfluid Host.

data_flows.data: Governance proposal transaction, vote transaction. (doc_11)
data_flows.description: DAO Participant (via User Wallet) interacts with SuperfluidGovernanceII contract.

data_flows.data: Authorized call to upgrade Host or Agreement logic, or change parameters. (doc_11, doc_61)
data_flows.description: SuperfluidGovernanceII executes a successful proposal on the Superfluid Host or other managed contracts.

trust_boundaries.name: User Domain
trust_boundaries.description: Boundary between the human user and their wallet software/hardware. Trust in wallet to act on user's behalf.

trust_boundaries.name: User-DApp Interface
trust_boundaries.description: Boundary between the user's wallet and the DApp/Client Application. DApp is untrusted from user's perspective until verified. Wallet prompts for transaction signing.

trust_boundaries.name: DApp/Wallet to Blockchain Network
trust_boundaries.description: Boundary where DApp/Client Application or User Wallet submits transactions to the Berachain network, interacting with Superfluid smart contracts (Forwarders, Tokens, Factory, Macros, TOGA, Governance). Transactions are validated by the blockchain.

trust_boundaries.name: Superfluid Core Protocol Boundary
trust_boundaries.description: Internal boundary encompassing the core Superfluid smart contracts (Host, Agreements, Tokens, Factory, TOGA, Pools, Macros, Governance, NFTs). These contracts operate with high trust in each other's defined interfaces and logic.

trust_boundaries.name: Protocol-SuperApp Interface
trust_boundaries.description: Boundary between the Superfluid Host contract and registered Super Apps. Callbacks are made from Host to Super App, and Super App can call back into Host.

trust_boundaries.name: On-chain/Off-chain Boundary
trust_boundaries.description: Boundary separating on-chain Superfluid smart contracts from off-chain services like Sentinel Node Software, Superfluid Subgraph, and DApp/Client Applications. Off-chain services read from or submit transactions to on-chain contracts.

trust_boundaries.name: Governance Boundary
trust_boundaries.description: Boundary where Superfluid Governance can enact changes (e.g., upgrades, parameter updates) to core protocol contracts.

behaviors.name: User Creates Money Stream
behaviors.description: A User, via their Wallet and a DApp, initiates a transaction to create a new money stream using a Super Token. (doc_6, doc_13, doc_34, doc_50)

behaviors.name: User Wraps ERC20 to Super Token
behaviors.description: A User, via their Wallet and a DApp, converts an Underlying ERC20 Token into its corresponding Wrapper Super Token. (doc_7, doc_55)

behaviors.name: User Unwraps Super Token to ERC20
behaviors.description: A User, via their Wallet and a DApp, converts a Wrapper Super Token back to its Underlying ERC20 Token. (doc_7, doc_55)

behaviors.name: Pool Admin Creates Distribution Pool
behaviors.description: A Pool Admin, via their Wallet and a DApp, creates a new Superfluid Distribution Pool for a specific Super Token. (doc_5, doc_28, doc_47)

behaviors.name: Pool Admin/User Distributes to Pool
behaviors.description: A Pool Admin or authorized User, via their Wallet and a DApp, distributes Super Tokens (instantly or as a stream) to a Distribution Pool. (doc_5, doc_29, doc_47)

behaviors.name: User Claims from Distribution Pool
behaviors.description: A User (pool member), via their Wallet and a DApp, claims their accrued share of Super Tokens from a Distribution Pool. (doc_46)

behaviors.name: Sentinel Liquidates Insolvent Stream
behaviors.action: Submit liquidation transaction to Superfluid Host (targeting CFAv1)
behaviors.description: A Sentinel, using its Node Software, identifies an insolvent stream and submits a transaction to liquidate it, claiming the buffer. (doc_18, doc_19)

behaviors.name: Super App Reacts to Stream Event
behaviors.description: The Superfluid Host contract invokes a registered callback function on a Super App when a relevant Superfluid event occurs (e.g., a stream is created to the Super App). (doc_3, doc_21, doc_24)

behaviors.name: PIC Stakes in TOGA
behaviors.action: Call staking function on TOGA Contract
behaviors.description: A PIC, via their Wallet, stakes Super Tokens in the TOGA contract to become eligible for liquidation rewards. (doc_18)

behaviors.name: Developer Deploys Super Token
behaviors.action: Call `createERC20Wrapper` or `deployNewPureSuperToken` on Super Token Factory
behaviors.description: A Developer, via their Wallet, deploys a new Super Token contract using the Super Token Factory. (doc_41, doc_42)

behaviors.name: Developer Deploys Super App
behaviors.action: Deploy Super App contract and register with Superfluid Host
behaviors.description: A Developer, via their Wallet, deploys a Super App smart contract and registers it with the Superfluid Host. (doc_22, doc_23)

behaviors.name: User Queries Superfluid Subgraph
behaviors.action: Send GraphQL query to Subgraph endpoint
behaviors.description: A User, typically via a DApp, queries the Superfluid Subgraph to retrieve indexed data about streams, pools, tokens, etc. (doc_48, doc_52)

behaviors.name: User Executes Batch Operation via Macro
behaviors.description: A User, via their Wallet and a DApp, executes a batch of Superfluid operations defined in a UserDefinedMacro contract through the MacroForwarder. (doc_45)

behaviors.name: Superfluid Governance Upgrades Protocol Contract
behaviors.action: Propose and vote on upgrade via SuperfluidGovernanceII
behaviors.description: Superfluid DAO Participants vote on a proposal, which, if successful, leads the SuperfluidGovernanceII contract to upgrade a core protocol component like the Host or an Agreement. (doc_11, doc_61)
